interactions.summary: Jiang extends Trump World into a Kabbalistic immortality theory: global attention focused on Trump may feed an ambition to become a cybernetic God-emperor figure.
The host asks what practical implication the Board of Peace has if Trump can veto it, Palestinians are excluded, and no two-state solution appears.
Jiang says the Board of Peace would replace the UN Security Council above a fracturing world marked by deindustrialization, mercantilist supply systems, and resource wars over water and energy.
The host asks whether this Board of Peace/American empire project will work.

Jiang says it will work because Trump is creating an American empire distinct from Pax Americana: colonies, territories, vassal states, technocracy, and expansionist interest in Greenland, Canada, Mexico, and Cuba.

speaker_notes.note: Both host questions are performed and sharply shape Jiang's institutional answer.

claims.claim: Jiang says modern communications concentrate humanity's attention on Trump in a historically novel way, feeding a Kabbalistic ambition toward longevity or immortality.
Jiang predicts the Iran/Middle East war will accelerate deindustrialization, mercantilist self-sufficiency, and resource wars, especially around water.
Jiang distinguishes Pax Americana from Trump's American empire: the former guaranteed rules-based trade, while the latter is a normal empire of colonies, vassals, territories, and technocratic rule.

signature_moments.moment: Trump's political ambition becomes metaphysical: God-emperor Trump powered by global attention.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It condenses Jiang's attention-power metaphysics into the Trump case.

signature_moments.moment: Pax Americana gives way to plain empire: colonies, vassals, and technocracy replace rules.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It clarifies a major distinction in Jiang's empire vocabulary.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The rules-based order in which America guarantees peace and trade through institutions like the WTO.
Trump's projected order of colonies, territories, vassal states, and technocratic rule, distinct from Pax Americana.
A state strategy of self-sufficiency and secured supply lines under fracturing global trade.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang anticipates Japan seeking a mercantile supply system and invokes the twentieth-century Japanese prosperity sphere as precedent.

uncertainty_notes.note: The Kabbalistic immortality account is explicitly speculative interpretation, not a factual claim the packet can verify.
